title: Sarga 104
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “The sage then began, asking Rama to listen, and revealed that he had been sent by the divine grandfather Brahma for a specific purpose.” through “Having created me from your divine, sun-like lotus navel, you entrusted all the work of creation to me.” through “Formerly, you yourself fixed the duration of your dwelling (on earth) for ten thousand and ten hundred years.”

Themes: devotion, mind and meditation, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “O best among men, you, the mind-born son, have completed your lifespan among humans here, and now it is time for you to return to my proximity.” through “Having heard the words spoken by the grandfather, which indicated the end of his time, Rama smiled and spoke words signifying the dissolution of…” through “You are indeed understood and your time has arrived, there is no doubt in my mind about that; for I must indeed act in all duties, obedient to…”

verses.sanskrit: शृणु राजन्महासत्व यदर्थमहमागतः । पितामहेन देवेन प्रेषितो ऽस्मि महाबल ।। 7.104.1 ।।
verses.translation: The sage then began, asking Rama to listen, and revealed that he had been sent by the divine grandfather Brahma for a specific purpose.

verses.sanskrit: तवाहं पूर्वसद्भावे पुत्रः परपुरञ्जय । मायासम्भावितो वीर कालः सर्वसमाहरः ।। 7.104.2 ।।
verses.translation: O conqueror of enemy cities, O hero, I am your son from a previous existence, created by your divine power as Time, the destroyer of all.

verses.sanskrit: पितामहश्च भगवानाह लोकपतिः प्रभुः । समयस्ते कृतः सौम्य लोकान्सम्परिरक्षितुम् ।। 7.104.3 ।।
verses.translation: And the revered Grandfather Brahma, the mighty lord of the worlds, said, "O gentle one, an agreement has been made by you to protect the worlds well."

verses.sanskrit: सङ्क्षिप्य हि पुरा लोकान्मायया स्वयमेव हि । महार्णवे शयानो ऽप्सु मां त्वं पूर्वमजीजनः ।। 7.104.4 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, formerly, having dissolved the worlds by your own divine power, you lay in the great ocean and begot me in the waters.

verses.sanskrit: भोगवन्तं ततो नागमनन्तमुदकेशयम् । मायया जनयित्वा त्वं द्वौ च सत्त्वौ महाबलौ ।। 7.104.5 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having created by your divine power the hooded serpent Ananta, who lies in the water, you also created two very powerful beings.

verses.sanskrit: मधुं च कैटभं चैव ययोरस्थिचयैर्वृता । इयं पर्वतसम्बाधा मेदिनी चाभवन्मही ।। 7.104.6 ।।
verses.translation: These two were Madhu and Kaitabha, whose heaps of bones covered this earth, which then became the great earth crowded with mountains.

verses.sanskrit: पद्मे दिव्ये ऽर्कसङ्काशे नाभ्यामुत्पाद्य मामपि । प्राजापत्यं त्वया कर्म मयि सर्वं निवेशितम् ।। 7.104.7 ।।
verses.translation: Having created me from your divine, sun-like lotus navel, you entrusted all the work of creation to me.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽहं सन्न्यस्तभारो हि त्वामुपासे जगत्पतिम् । रक्षां विधत्स्व भूतेषु मम तेजस्करो भवान् ।। 7.104.8 ।।
verses.translation: Having indeed relinquished that burden, I now worship you, the Lord of the world; please bestow protection upon all beings and increase my splendor.
Therefore, you also, from that unassailable and eternal state, assumed the form of Vishnu for the protection of all beings.

verses.sanskrit: अदित्यां वीर्यवान्पुत्रो भ्रातऽणां वीर्यवर्धनः । समुत्पन्नेषु कृत्येषु तेषां साह्याय कल्पसे ।। 7.104.10 ।।
verses.translation: As a powerful son born to Aditi, increasing the valor of your brothers, you become capable of assisting them when difficulties arise.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं वित्रास्यमानासु प्रजासु जगतां वर । रावणस्य वधाकाङ्क्षी मानुषेषु मनो ऽदधाः ।। 7.104.11 ।।
verses.translation: O best of the worlds, when the creatures were being terrified, you, desiring the slaying of Ravana, set your mind on being born among humans.

verses.sanskrit: दश वर्षसहस्राणि दश वर्षशतानि च । कृत्वा वासस्य नियतिं स्वयमेवात्मना पुरा ।। 7.104.12 ।।
verses.translation: Formerly, you yourself fixed the duration of your dwelling (on earth) for ten thousand and ten hundred years.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं मनोमयः पुत्रः पूर्णायुर्मानुषेष्विह । कालो ऽयं ते नरश्रेष्ठ समीपमुपवर्तितुम् ।। 7.104.13 ।।
verses.translation: O best among men, you, the mind-born son, have completed your lifespan among humans here, and now it is time for you to return to my proximity.

verses.sanskrit: यदि भूयो महाराज प्रजा इच्छस्युपासितुम् । वस वा वीर भद्रं त एवमाह पितामहः ।। 7.104.14 ।।
verses.translation: The grandfather (Brahma) said, "O great king, if you desire to rule your subjects again, or if you wish to stay, O hero, may good fortune be upon you."

verses.sanskrit: अथ वा विजिगीषा ते सुरलोकाय राघव । सनाथा विष्णुना देवा भवन्तु विगतज्वराः ।। 7.104.15 ।।
verses.translation: Or, O Raghava, if you desire to return to the world of the gods, then may the gods be protected by Vishnu and be free from all distress.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा पितामहेनोक्तं वाक्यं कालसमीरितम् । राघवः प्रहसन्वाक्यं सर्वसंहारमब्रवीत् ।। 7.104.16 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the words spoken by the grandfather, which indicated the end of his time, Rama smiled and spoke words signifying the dissolution of everything.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा मे देवदेवस्य वाक्यं परममद्भुतम् । प्रीतिर्हि महती जाता तवागमनसम्भवा ।। 7.104.17 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the supreme and wonderful words of the god of gods, a great joy has indeed arisen in me, caused by your arrival.

verses.sanskrit: त्रयाणामपि लोकानां कार्यार्थं मम सम्भवः । भद्रं ते ऽस्तु गमिष्यामि यत एवाहमागतः ।। 7.104.18 ।।
verses.translation: My existence is for the purpose of the three worlds; may good fortune be to you, I shall now depart to the place from where I came.

verses.sanskrit: हद्गतो ह्यसि सम्प्राप्तो न मे तत्र विचारणा । मया हि सर्वकृत्येषु देवानां वशवर्तिनाम् । स्थातव्यं सर्वसंहार यथा ह्याह पितामहः ।। 7.104.19 ।।
verses.translation: You are indeed understood and your time has arrived, there is no doubt in my mind about that; for I must indeed act in all duties, obedient to the gods, for the destruction of all, just as the grandfather Brahma has indeed commanded. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे चतुरधिकशततमः सर्गः ।। 104 ।। Thus ends the hundred and fourth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
